item_name: EU Digital Fairness Act Dark-Pattern Audit
definition_origin: The EU Digital Fairness Act (DFA) is a forthcoming EU regulation announced in the European Commission's 2024-2025 Commission work programme as a successor to the Digital Services Act (DSA, in force February 2024). The legislative proposal is expected Q4 2026, with adoption likely 2027-2028. Built on the Digital Fairness Fitness Check (2024 Commission report) and European Parliament resolution of 12 December 2023 on addictive design, the DFA explicitly targets: dark patterns, addictive design, manipulative consent UIs, subscription/cancellation traps, deceptive personalization, and AI-driven persuasion. Earlier scaffolding: DSA Article 25 (in force Feb 2024 — prohibits online interface designs that 'deceive or manipulate'); BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) — first major dark-pattern enforcement signal under DSA; Goodwin Law alert November 2025 on EU Commission's 'fair play' framing. The Petrie-Flom Center (Harvard Law, August 2025) frames the DFA as 'a new chapter in EU public health policy' citing The Lancet Psychiatry Commission 2025 global initiative on addictive design.
core_mechanism: Five regulatory mechanisms targeted. (1) DARK PATTERNS — codified taxonomy from deceptive.design (Brignull): confirm-shaming, sludge (asymmetric subscribe vs cancel friction), preselection / opt-out checkboxes, fake urgency / countdown timers, low-stock indicators that aren't real-time, hidden costs revealed at checkout. (2) ADDICTIVE DESIGN — variable-ratio reinforcement (pull-to-refresh, slot-machine streak risk), infinite scroll without natural stopping cue, autoplay without opt-in, push notification volume, dopamine-reward loops. (3) MANIPULATIVE CONSENT UIs — Reject All harder than Accept All (CNIL precedent), bundled consent (GDPR Art 7 violation), pre-checked boxes (Art 4(11) violation). (4) DECEPTIVE PERSONALIZATION — algorithmic targeting of vulnerable users (detected loneliness, addiction patterns), AI-driven persuasion that adapts to weakness. (5) MINOR PROTECTIONS — extends UK Children's Code Standard 13 (nudge techniques) to EU-wide; engagement-maximizing UI to under-18 prohibited. Onboarding-specific application: every onboarding decision (questionnaire framing, paywall design, consent UI, sequencing, microcopy) must pre-audit against this taxonomy. The 2026 timeline urgency: apps shipping in EU should pre-audit NOW because retrofitting after the DFA's expected 2027-2028 adoption is harder than building compliant from start.
neuroscience_correlate: DFA is grounded in neuroscience: variable-ratio reinforcement activates VTA→NAcc dopamine system (Schultz 1997); intermittent rewards produce strongest persistence (Skinner). Lancet Psychiatry Commission 2025 explicitly invokes addiction neuroscience to justify regulating addictive design like tobacco / gambling. Petrie-Flom Center frames DFA as public health regulation comparable to smoking legislation.
empirical_evidence: DSA Article 25 enforcement (in force Feb 2024): EU investigations against Meta, Temu, X, AliExpress, SHEIN. DSA Observatory (Aug 2025): six major platforms still using manipulative design despite DSA. BEUC SHEIN complaint (5 June 2025): filed by BEUC + 25 consumer organizations from 21 EU countries; identified 8 distinct dark patterns on SHEIN — low-stock messages, countdown timers ('Flash sale'), peer-pressure testimonials, confirm-shaming, infinite scroll, nagging notifications, emotional manipulation, false urgency. BEUC quote (Director-General Agustin Reyna): 'SHEIN is designed to be addictive: driven by powerful algorithms to maximise consumer engagement and over-spending.' European Parliament resolution 12 December 2023 (NRG 2024/2027(INI)): non-addictive-by-default design, restrictions on infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications. EPRS Briefing 2025 (PE 767.191): regulatory roadmap. Petrie-Flom Center (Harvard Law, August 2025): 'Addictive Algorithms and the Digital Fairness Act — A New Chapter in EU Public Health Policy.' Goodwin Law November 2025 alert: 'From Dark Patterns to Fair Play — EU Commission's Direction.' Cristiana Santos et al. (SSRN 2024): empirical taxonomy of platforms and patterns falling under DSA Article 25. CNIL 2024-2025 enforcement on cookie banners (€100M+ aggregate fines).
measurable_outcome_metric: Dark-pattern audit count (using deceptive.design taxonomy, target = 0). Confirm-shaming exit modal count (target = 0). Asymmetric subscribe vs cancel ratio (target ≤ 1.0). Addictive-design feature count (variable-ratio, infinite scroll, autoplay) — declining trend. Push notification volume per user (declining). Manipulative consent count (Reject All harder than Accept) (target = 0). Pre-DFA audit pass rate (qualitative).
evidence_strength: legal_requirement_pending (DFA proposal Q4 2026; current DSA Article 25 already enforceable; UK CMA Subscription Contracts Act 2024 already in force; UK Children's Code already in force)
conversion_lift_benchmark: Not a conversion-lift item — defensive compliance + ethical-design imperative. Negative outcome avoidance: DSA fines up to 6% of global annual turnover (VLOPs/VLOSEs). DSA enforcement actions ongoing against Meta, Temu, X, AliExpress, SHEIN. EU DFA Q4 2026 will add regulatory layer; UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 (in force April 2026) imports similar standards. Long-tail: ethical-design choices that comply with DFA also align with archetype-positive brand voice (per emotional-design research) — so compliance creates brand-equity lift in conscious-consumer markets.
engineering_tactics: PRE-DFA AUDIT CHECKLIST (use Claude as audit tool): (1) PAYWALL audit — no fake countdowns, no fake low-stock badges, no confirm-shaming exit modals, no asymmetric subscribe vs cancel friction (cancel must be ≤ subscribe taps), no hidden trial-to-paid conversion, no toggle paywalls (banned 2026-01 by Apple 3.1.2 separately). (2) CONSENT UI audit — no bundled Accept All, no pre-checked boxes, Reject All button of EQUAL prominence to Accept (CNIL precedent), per-purpose toggles. (3) NOTIFICATIONS audit — no engagement-maximizing volume, no re-prompt loops, day-2 push must serve user-stated goals (SDT-aligned), opt-in via pre-permission soft-ask not cold native. (4) STREAKS audit — restoration grace days for free users, no streak-shame messaging, no 'Are you sure you want to break your streak?' confirm-shaming. (5) INFINITE SCROLL audit — natural stopping cue (e.g. 'You're all caught up'), no autoplay-next without explicit opt-in. (6) PERSONALIZATION audit — no algorithmic targeting of vulnerable users (detected loneliness, addiction); no AI persuasion that adapts to weakness. (7) MINORS audit (UK Children's Code Standard 13 + DFA expected) — high-privacy default, no nudges, age-appropriate language, parental info link prominent. (8) CANCELLATION audit (FTC + UK SCA 2024) — symmetric to subscribe path, no retention-funnel before cancel API, no double-negative button copy. Implementation: build a dark-pattern audit script that scans React Native code for known antipatterns: const audit = await runDFAAudit({ paywallComponents, consentUI, notifications, streakLogic, scrollComponents }); failures.forEach(f => log.warn(f)). Run pre-deploy + nightly.
real_world_examples: 1) BEUC SHEIN complaint (June 2025) — most-manipulative trader, 8 dark patterns; BENCHMARK FOR WHAT NOT TO DO. 2) Meta — DSA Article 25 investigation 2024-2026 for cookie-consent + ad-personalization opt-out dark patterns. 3) Temu — DSA investigation 2024-2025; gamified shopping, fake countdowns, manipulated reviews. 4) TikTok — autoplay, infinite scroll, push volume — flagged in EP addictive-design resolution. 5) X — DSA Article 25 investigation re: algorithmic amplification + verification dark pattern. 6) Duolingo — streak shame mechanism cited by Center for Humane Technology and design ethics literature. 7) Snapchat Streaks — flagged by child-safety researchers as exemplar social-pressure addictive design. 8) Apple Screen Time — COMPLIANT counter-example: platform-level mitigations that don't extract attention. 9) Apple Notification Summary — COMPLIANT: reduces notification overload. 10) Center for Humane Technology design framework — humane-tech alternative to addictive design. 11) Compliant-onboarding example: Headspace / Calm — gentle pacing, no streak shame, no confirm-shaming exit, transparent consent UI.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile carries greater DFA risk: (a) push notifications are uniquely mobile attention vector explicitly named in EP addictive-design resolution; (b) infinite scroll on mobile has no scrollbar / page-end cue; (c) haptic feedback intensifies dopamine engagement; (d) age verification harder on mobile; (e) ATT framework adds consent layer. Web has more sludge / cookie-banner exposure under ePrivacy + GDPR. App stores (Apple, Google) add second compliance layer.
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass / M3 Expressive are morally neutral — used for compliant or for dark-pattern design. Apple platform-level mitigations (Focus modes, Notification Summary, Screen Time) align with DFA. Android Digital Wellbeing equivalent. Aligning with platform-level wellness tools = positive DFA compliance signal.
category_fit: Universal — any app shipping in EU. Especially critical for: high-engagement apps (social, gaming, video, streaming — addictive-design risk), wellness/habit (streak mechanics + push notifications), AI-wrapper (AI persuasion risk), kids/family (minor protections), e-commerce (BEUC SHEIN precedent). Lower-stakes: pure utility one-shot apps (less dark-pattern surface area).
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for pre-DFA audit + remediation of identified violations. ~2 days run audit checklist (Claude-assisted code review). ~3 days remediate findings (remove fake countdowns, fix consent UI symmetry, add streak-restoration grace, add infinite-scroll stopping cue). ~1 day documentation + audit log for regulator-readiness. Solo-dev shortcut: build compliant from start (no fake urgency, symmetric consent + cancel, gentle streak design) — much cheaper than retrofitting later.
tooling_options: deceptive.design audit taxonomy (free, comprehensive). Center for Humane Technology design framework (free). Anthropic Claude as audit assistant (custom prompt + code review). DSA Observatory tracker (free, EU enforcement intelligence). EPRS legislative train (DFA tracker). UK ICO Children's Code self-assessment toolkit. Datafemme / Privacy International tools. PostHog session-recording for behavior audit (DFA evidence trail). Sentry / Datadog for telemetry on dark-pattern indicators (e.g., spike in cancel-flow dropoff = sludge alert).
ai_dependency: optional. Claude / GPT-4 as dark-pattern audit assistant — promp the model with deceptive.design taxonomy + the codebase, get a violations list with file:line references and compliant rewrites. NOT required — audit can be manual.
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Audit this React Native onboarding + paywall + cancellation flow for EU Digital Fairness Act compliance. Use deceptive.design taxonomy. Identify violations of: (a) confirm-shaming, (b) sludge / asymmetric subscribe-cancel, (c) preselection / pre-checked boxes, (d) fake urgency / countdown timers, (e) hidden costs, (f) infinite scroll without stopping cue, (g) autoplay without opt-in, (h) streak shame, (i) Reject All harder than Accept All, (j) addictive notification volume, (k) AI persuasion targeting vulnerability, (l) under-18 engagement-maximizing nudges. For each violation, output: file:line reference, taxonomy category, regulatory exposure (DSA Art 25 / DFA pending / UK Children Code / FTC + state), compliant rewrite, severity (critical / moderate / minor). Prioritize critical findings.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Refactor this paywall to remove identified DFA violations: replace fake countdown with real-eligibility-based offer; symmetric subscribe-cancel pathway (cancel ≤ subscribe taps); remove confirm-shaming exit; per-purpose consent toggles; Reject All button of equal prominence to Accept; transparent material terms ≥14pt body, not 8pt grey. Cite DFA / DSA / UK SCA 2024 / FTC provisions in code comments for audit trail.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate three variants of a subscription cancellation flow that comply with DSA Article 25 + UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 + DFA pending. All three variants must: (a) present cancel as primary action with same visual weight as continue, (b) complete in ≤2 taps, (c) NOT include retention modals that confirm-shame, (d) NOT preselect a discount upsell, (e) include success state with immediate confirmation. Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope. Forbid: fake countdown badges, double-negative button copy.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate a DFA-compliant onboarding paywall: NO fake countdown, NO low-stock badge, NO confirm-shaming exit, NO toggle paywall (Apple 3.1.2 ban). Side-by-side cards (3 plans max), per-card CTA, transparent timeline disclosure (Today: free / Day 4: billed / Cancel anytime), Reject-this-paywall close button of equal prominence to subscribe CTA. Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope. Cite DFA pending + DSA Article 25 in code comments.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen actively generates dark patterns as defaults because training data is full of them: (1) Confirm-shaming exit modals ('Wait! Don't go yet') as default exit handler. (2) Pre-checked marketing-opt-in boxes on signup. (3) Countdown timers + low-stock indicators on e-commerce/paywall prompts. (4) Asymmetric subscribe-cancel paths (subscribe one tap, cancel buried 4 levels deep). (5) Infinite scroll on any list prompt. (6) Push-notification permission re-prompt loops. (7) Streak shame mechanics ('You broke your 7-day streak!'). Counter-prompt: 'Forbid all dark patterns from deceptive.design taxonomy. Symmetric paths: if subscribe is N taps, cancel is N taps. No preselected opt-ins. No countdown timers unless real and verified eligibility. No confirm-shaming language. Streak restoration grace days for free users. Reject All button of equal prominence to Accept. Cite DSA Article 25 + DFA pending + UK Children's Code in reasoning when in doubt. evidence_strength: legal_requirement_pending (DFA Q4 2026 draft).'
anti_patterns: 1) Confirm-shaming exit modals. 2) Preselected upsells / consent. 3) Asymmetric subscribe vs cancel friction (sludge). 4) Fake countdown timers and low-stock messages. 5) Infinite scroll without user-initiated stopping cue. 6) Autoplay without opt-in. 7) Push-notification re-prompt loops. 8) Streak shame mechanics. 9) Hidden costs revealed at final step. 10) Pre-ticked third-party data-sharing boxes. 11) Engagement-maximizing notification volume to under-18. 12) AI persuasion targeting detected vulnerability. 13) Toggle paywalls (banned 2026-01 separately). 14) Reject All harder than Accept All (CNIL).
regulatory_risk: HIGH and rising. DSA Article 25 (in force Feb 2024) — max fine 6% global annual turnover (VLOPs/VLOSEs); enforcement against Meta, Temu, X, AliExpress, SHEIN ongoing. DSA Article 27 (recommender system transparency). DSA Article 38 (recommender system options for VLOPs). UCPD (Unfair Commercial Practices Directive) — basis for SHEIN BEUC complaint. UK Consumer Protection from Unfair Trading Regulations 2008. UK ICO Children's Code Standard 13 (Nudge techniques). UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 (April 2026). EU DFA Q4 2026 draft — extends to addictive design, dopamine loops, autoplay, infinite scroll, AI persuasion. CNIL France 2024-2025 cookie-banner enforcement (€100M+). For any app with streaks, notifications, variable rewards, in-app currency, social pressure, FOMO, or under-18 users: assume DFA will apply.
platform_constraints_2026: DSA Article 25 (in force Feb 2024) — direct enforcement. UK ICO Children's Code Standard 13 (in force Sept 2021). UK Subscription Contracts Act 2024 (in force April 2026). Apple App Store Guideline 4.5.4 — push prohibition for marketing without opt-in. Apple iOS 26+ Focus Modes + Notification Summary (platform mitigations). Google Play Digital Wellbeing equivalent. EU DFA Q4 2026 draft expected; compliance window 2027-2028 estimated.
accessibility_implications: DFA + accessibility align: addictive design (motion-only triggers, autoplay, no-stopping-cue scroll) violates WCAG 2.2 SC 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions) + SC 2.2.2 (Pause Stop Hide). Streak shame may violate SC 3.3.5 (Help). Symmetric subscribe-cancel aligns with SC 3.2.5 (Change on Request). DFA pre-audit + WCAG 2.2 audit overlap significantly — single audit pass covers both.
pitfalls_caveats: 1) Line between persuasion and manipulation contested — even good-faith design can be flagged later. 2) Cultural calibration: confirm-shaming reads worse to Northern European users than US — default-off is safest globally. 3) UK Children's Code applies to any service likely accessed by under-18s, regardless of stated audience. 4) Many onboarding 'nudges' that feel benign (streak warnings, social-proof testimonials) are within DFA emerging scope. 5) AI personalization adapting to vulnerable users explicitly singled out as DFA target. 6) DFA timeline uncertainty — Q4 2026 proposal could slip; build for current DSA + UK + member-state laws as floor. 7) Brussels effect — extraterritorial reach means non-EU apps still must comply.
cross_cultural_validity: EU regulation has extraterritorial effect (Brussels effect). UK has parallel regime (CMA + ICO). US has no federal equivalent yet but California (CCPA + AADC), Utah, Connecticut, Texas moving. China PIPL + Cyberspace Administration regulations have parallel addictive-design rules for minors (gaming time limits, Douyin under-18 mode). Japan weaker dark-pattern regulation but strong app-store self-regulation. Apply EU + UK as strictest baseline globally.
prescribed_action: Run a DFA pre-audit NOW using deceptive.design taxonomy as checklist. (1) Audit paywall: remove fake countdowns / low-stock / confirm-shaming exit; ensure symmetric subscribe-cancel (cancel ≤ subscribe taps); explicit material terms ≥14pt; no toggle paywall (Apple 3.1.2 separately banned). (2) Audit consent UI: Reject All of EQUAL prominence to Accept; per-purpose toggles (not bundled); no pre-checked boxes; default-off for EU. (3) Audit notifications: no re-prompt loops; serve user-stated goals (SDT); pre-permission soft-ask not cold native. (4) Audit streaks: restoration grace days for free users; no shame messaging; no confirm-shaming break. (5) Audit infinite scroll: natural stopping cue ('You're all caught up'); no autoplay-next without opt-in. (6) Audit personalization: no algorithmic targeting of detected vulnerability; no AI persuasion adapting to weakness. (7) Audit minors: high-privacy default, no nudges, age-appropriate language. (8) Document audit results for regulator-readiness audit trail. Mark evidence_strength: legal_requirement_pending. Build compliant from start — retrofit cost after DFA adoption is materially higher.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 02_design (compliant paywall + consent UI + streak design), 04_implementation (audit script + telemetry trail), 03_architecture (state-machine routing for symmetric paths), 01_psychology (cross-reference to ethical persuasion boundary).
questionnaire_relevance: Questionnaire framing must NOT exploit detected vulnerability (detected loneliness, depression, addiction-prone users). Question types should not include manipulative-personalization triggers. Privacy notice + consent UI before questionnaire (not bundled).
aha_relevance: Aha quality + DFA compliance align: a strong genuine Aha (real value delivered) doesn't need dark-pattern crutches. Reliance on fake urgency, confirm-shaming, sludge signals weak Aha — fix the value first.
telemetry_events: dfa_audit_run (audit_score, violations_count), dark_pattern_detected (taxonomy_category, file_line), confirm_shaming_modal_shown (suppress for compliance), streak_restoration_grace_offered, infinite_scroll_stopping_cue_shown, consent_reject_all_clicked (vs accept_all), persistent_cta_addictive_indicator, notification_re_prompt_loop_detected (suppress).
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (audit pass at deploy gate). Ongoing (nightly audit script; new dark patterns introduced via dependency updates flagged). Annual (compliance audit + regulator-readiness check). 2027-2028 (DFA adoption — adjust audit checklist).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (gentle, transparent design = ultimate care), Sage (informed honest disclosure), Innocent (no manipulation = simple trust). Conflicts with Outlaw / Magician / Hero archetypes IF those interpret regulatory friction as anti-flow — but compliance is non-negotiable. Pairs strongly with Ethical Persuasion Boundary (.agent/research/emotional-design/results/Ethical_Persuasion_Boundary.json) and Dark Patterns DFA (.agent/research/emotional-design/results/Dark_Patterns_DFA.json).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against the ENTIRE deceptive.design taxonomy: Confirm-Shaming, Sludge, Pre-Checked Boxes, Fake Urgency, Hidden Costs, Infinite Scroll No Stop, Autoplay No Opt-In, Streak Shame, Push Re-Prompt Loop, Pre-Ticked Sharing, Engagement-Maximizing Minor Nudges, AI Persuasion Vulnerability Targeting. Aligns with: GDPR Data Minimization (item 46), CMP / ATT Framework (item 47), COPPA Age Gate (item 48), Click-to-Cancel Compliance (item 50), Hard Paywall Climax (item 34, ethical variant), Story Onboarding Doctrine (item 18, value-first not manipulation-first).
sources: [EPRS Briefing 2025: Regulating Dark Patterns in the EU](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/RegData/etudes/ATAG/2025/767191/EPRS_ATA(2025)767191_EN.pdf)

[Petrie-Flom Center: Addictive Algorithms and the Digital Fairness Act](https://petrieflom.law.harvard.edu/2025/08/20/addictive-algorithms-and-the-digital-fairness-act-a-new-chapter-in-eu-public-health-policy/)
[Goodwin Law: From Dark Patterns to Fair Play (Nov 2025)](https://www.goodwinlaw.com/en/insights/publications/2025/11/alerts-practices-antc-from-dark-patterns-to-fair-play)
